title: trymalium myrtillus subsp. pungens
intro: Trymalium myrtillus subsp. pungens, also known as the prickly myrtle, is a shrub-like plant species that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been employed to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Trymalium myrtillus subsp. pungens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
